compiled_state.title: Agent dispatches dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch without required sonnet sub-agent findings — violates deep-tier review protocol
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

During the review escalation for bug b5e2-56ad, the orchestrator dispatched dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch directly (as the sole reviewer), without first running the 3 parallel sonnet specialists (dso:code-reviewer-deep-correctness, dso:code-reviewer-deep-verification, dso:code-reviewer-deep-hygiene). The deep-arch agent is a synthesis agent — it requires the findings from the 3 sonnet specialists as input to produce a unified architectural review.

## Rationalization

The orchestrator treated dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch as a standalone 'opus-level reviewer' rather than as the synthesis layer of the deep-tier review pipeline. It dispatched deep-arch directly with the diff, expecting it to perform both specialist analysis AND synthesis in a single pass.

## Expected behavior

The deep tier review is a unified system (per CLAUDE.md architecture table and REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md Step 4 Deep Tier section):
1. 3 parallel sonnet agents write specialist findings (correctness, verification, hygiene)
2. dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch (opus) synthesizes those findings into a unified review
3. deep-arch is the SOLE writer of the final reviewer-findings.json

Dispatching deep-arch without sonnet findings violates the single-writer invariant and produces a review based on opus reading the diff alone rather than synthesizing specialist analysis.

## Fix needed

1. Strengthen the deep-arch agent definition to reject invocations without sonnet findings
2. Add explicit guidance in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md that deep-arch must NOT be dispatched standalone
3. This is related to bug 04ea-4234 which describes the same pattern during review escalation

comments.body: Behavioral assertion (Step 5 LLM exemption): After fix, reviewer-delta-deep-arch.md MUST contain a SONNET FINDINGS GUARD that rejects invocations without SONNET-A/B/C markers. Grep for 'SONNET.*GUARD' in the delta file.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
